title: melicope tiarapuensis
intro: Melicope tiarapuensis, also known as the Tiarapu melicope, is a plant species belonging to the Rutaceae family, native to Tahiti and French Polynesia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as coughs and colds, thanks to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's health benefits are attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation, alleviate pain, and soothe respiratory tract irritations. In herbal preparations, the leaves and bark of Melicope tiarapuensis are typically used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
